ai-w5-e1.answer: Describe 3 scenarios (lookup, sort, queue). Ask which Java collection. Justify each answer.
ai-w5-e1.realWorld: LRU cache → LinkedHashMap; priority tasks → PriorityQueue.

ai-w5-e2.answer: Compare ArrayList vs LinkedList with AI. Write micro-benchmark for get(5000) on 10k list.
ai-w5-e2.realWorld: ArrayList wins random access — measure proves textbook claim.

ai-w5-e3.answer: HashMap vs TreeMap: when need sorted keys? Write 5-line example of each.
ai-w5-e3.realWorld: TreeMap for range queries on keys; HashMap for plain speed.

ai-w5-e4.answer: Ask how HashSet avoids duplicates internally (hashCode, equals). Implement contains duplicate check both ways.
ai-w5-e4.realWorld: Understanding equals/hashCode contract prevents Set bugs with custom objects.

ai-w5-e5.answer: Review Student Management System for collection misuse. Fix one hot path.
ai-w5-e5.realWorld: Linear search in list on every login → Map by studentId.

ai-w5-e6.answer: Replace nested loop duplicate-finder with HashSet. Same results, time both.
ai-w5-e6.realWorld: Classic optimization exercise — appears in interviews and real code review.

ai-w5-e7.answer: Ask Big-O of your methods. Explain n² loop aloud in 30 seconds.
ai-w5-e7.realWorld: Interview: 'What's complexity of this nested for?' — practice here.

ai-w5-e8.answer: List edge tests for a method using collections. Implement 3 as JUnit or main asserts.
ai-w5-e8.realWorld: Empty list, null list, list with null element — define expected behavior.

ai-w5-e9.answer: Paste code using Vector or Hashtable. Ask for modern alternative. Migrate if safe.
ai-w5-e9.realWorld: Legacy synchronized collections → ArrayList + Collections.synchronizedList or concurrent types.

ai-w5-e10.answer: Ask memory difference: ArrayList of 1M Integers vs int[]. Discuss boxing cost.
ai-w5-e10.realWorld: Production lesson: primitives in tight memory paths matter at scale.
